title: Remote Pod: request NVMe disk + investigate REST disk-type control
description: App's _createPodInternal (routes/remoteProxy.js) never sets a disk type, so RunPod places Pods on whatever host storage is free (often slower SSD). NVMe is meaningfully faster for model load (big Wan/Flux weights). The RunPod console's NVMe/SSD toggle is a HOST-AVAILABILITY filter on the GPU picker, not a documented Pod-spec field — so the fix is likely a datacenter/host filter on the create path, not a 'diskType' field. Investigate whether RunPod REST /pods accepts any disk-type hint; if not, filter the GPU/DC list to NVMe-capable hosts. No image rebuild needed (deploy-time/host concern, not baked in the image). Applies to both product Pods and the new cubric-vision-builder authoring image. App. See brief.md.
